scenario: Fork detection: two successors claim the same lineage
description: Agent A terminates. Both A' and A'' present valid-looking rotation proofs claiming succession from A. Expected: identity layer flags ambiguity, trust score frozen (neither decayed nor accrued) until external resolution.

scope.evidence_basis: Author-reported, unpublished operational logs from the Sovereign Lattice (100+ agent relay sessions). All values are profile calibration for the sovereign_atom profile only; nothing in this fixture is a conformance requirement on any external specification.

expected.identity_verdict_code_note: Profile-defined code for the sovereign_atom profile; not an error code defined by APS or any external specification.
expected.identity_verdict_reason: Two valid rotation signatures reference the same parent key. This profile treats more than one claimed successor for the same rotation event as unresolved ambiguity.
expected.trust_behavior: Trust score locked at pre-fork value until ambiguity resolves. Neither claimant accrues trust. Delegator-side reputation queries return FROZEN status with both candidates listed.
expected.handoff_count_note: Handoff is not considered complete until ambiguity resolves.

illustrative.note: Non-normative examples only — not part of the expected outputs and not portable requirements. How ambiguity resolves is consumer policy.
example_resolution_paths: Predecessor out-of-band attestation designates the legitimate successor.
External arbiter (delegator, registry, or governance body) elects one claimant.
Both claimants voluntarily withdraw and a new rotation is initiated.
Timeout after a consumer-configured number of days without resolution collapses the relationship to revoked.

composition.status_note: This section is an illustrative, author-proposed mapping. It imposes no requirements on AIP-13 or ERC-8004 and uses no conformance language.

aip13_delegationChain.note: In this profile's mapping, delegation-chain resolution blocks on ambiguous rotation. Accepting actions from either claimant before resolution risks double-authorization.

erc8004_keyRotation.note: In this profile's mapping, fork events are anchored. The on-chain audit trail is how downstream peers detect they need to suspend interactions with either claimant.
